title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy scalp
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy scalp are natural remedies made from infusions of plants and herbs. They help alleviate itchiness by soothing the scalp, reducing inflammation, and fighting against fungal or bacterial infections. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for an itchy scalp include chamomile, nettle, and calendula. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce itching and irritation, while nettle is rich in nutrients that promote hair growth and soothe the scalp. Calendula, on the other hand, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help treat scalp infections and dandruff. By using herbal teas for an itchy scalp, individuals can experience relief from discomfort, improved scalp health, and enhanced hair growth, leading to an overall improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation that can cause itchiness and flaking. Additionally, chamomile's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to the itchiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment and relieving discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German Chamomile contains apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and soothes irritated skin, providing relief from itchiness on the scalp.
Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help combat oxidative stress and damage to the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Calming and sedative: German Chamomile has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related scalp itchiness, promoting relaxation and reduced scratching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps soothe and calm itchy skin.
Flowers: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol, which reduces redness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chamomile flowers according to traditional methods for optimal potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and preserving the herbal infusion.
Serve the chamomile tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial compounds help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, pot marigold teas contain antioxidants that promote healthy hair growth and strengthen the scalp, further reducing the occurrence of itchy scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can alleviate itching and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in pot marigold tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, inhibit the growth of fungi, bacteria, and other microorganisms that may be contributing to an itchy scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidant compounds help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used to make medicinal teas due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant are used to create teas that can help reduce itchiness and inflammation on the scalp by promoting relaxation and reducing redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers or use dried petals in small quantities as desired.
Measure one teaspoon of dried calendula petals or three teaspoons of fresh petals into a tea infuser.
Combine the calendula mixture with boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and enjoy immediately.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container refrigerated for up to three days or frozen indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with itchy scalp because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The tea's antimicrobial properties also help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender promote relaxation, which can alleviate stress-related scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching on the scalp.
Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties can combat fungal infections, such as dandruff and ringworm, that may be causing itchiness and discomfort on the scalp.
Sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender can help reduce stress and anxiety-related itchiness on the scalp, promoting a sense of relaxation and soothing relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential oils that help to calm itching and reduce inflammation, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers in the morning for optimal potency and aroma.
Combine one tablespoon of dried lavender flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing the calming essential oils into the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Serve the warm herbal tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, possess a calming effect on the skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, rosemary tea's antibacterial qualities help to eliminate fungal infections that can cause itchiness, such as dandruff and psoriasis. By soothing and balancing the scalp, rosemary teas provide relief from itchy scalp conditions, promoting a healthy and comfortable head environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and soothe itching caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in rosemary tea combat free radicals that can damage the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting healthy hair growth.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Rosemary tea's antibacterial and antifungal properties help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp that can cause itching, redness, and flaking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots are believed to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
Bark: Rosemary bark contains astringent properties that help to reduce dandruff and flakiness on the scalp, making it an effective ingredient for soothing itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rosemary leaves and measure out one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured rosemary with boiling water in a large teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove leaves.
Serve the tea hot, garnished with additional rosemary sprigs if desired, and enjoy within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with itchy scalp because of its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The herbs in thyme tea, such as thymol and carvacrol, work together to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation that can cause itchiness. Additionally, the antioxidants in thyme tea help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow to the scalp, further alleviating itchy sensations. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to tackle itchy scalps without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Thyme's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itching, redness, and flakiness on the scalp.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and irritation in the scalp, which can help to soothe itchy scalps and alleviate discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itching and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Bark: The bark of the thyme plant contains compounds that reduce itching and inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in teas for soothing itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves or one teaspoon of fresh leaves per eight ounces.
Bring filtered water to boiling point then reduce heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Add the thyme leaves to the hot water and allow it to steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help to control fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to an itchy scalp. By incorporating herbal lemon balm teas into one's routine, individuals can experience a decrease in itchiness and irritation, promoting a healthier and more comfortable scalp.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the scalp, which can alleviate itchiness and discomfort.
Calming and soothing effects: The essential oils in lemon balm, such as citral and linalool, have a calming effect on the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting relaxation.
Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness and flakiness on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain compounds that help soothe and calm the skin, making them effective in reducing itchiness and inflammation associated with an itchy scalp.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by itching, while also providing a calming effect on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves or one teaspoon of fresh leaves.
Heat water to a temperature between 195°f to 205°f and pour it over the measured herbs.
Steep the mixture for 7-10 minutes allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool to drinking temperature before consuming within 20 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The antioxidants present in sage tea help to soothe and calm the irritation, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, sage's natural antifungal agents work to combat fungal infections that can cause itchiness, such as dandruff and psoriasis. As a result, drinking sage teas may provide relief from an itchy scalp by addressing the underlying causes of the condition.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea's antibacterial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness in the scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to itchy scalps, promoting healthy scalp function and reducing inflammation.
Anti-pruritic properties: Sage tea contains compounds that specifically target itchiness, soothing and calming the skin and scalp to provide relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sage are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Bark: The bark of sage contains salicylic acid, a natural exfoliant that helps to remove dead skin cells and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sage leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Combine the sage leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Discard the solids and add honey or other sweetener to taste if desired one teaspoon per cup.
Pour the prepared sage tea into a cup and serve hot or chilled with lemon slices optional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with itchy scalp because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, work to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, redness, and flakiness on the scalp. Additionally, oregano's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-invasive way to promote healthy hair growth and alleviate scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Oregano tea's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections like ringworm, athlete's foot, and jock itch, which can cause itchy scalps.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in oregano tea reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
Antioxidant: Oregano's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to dryness, flakiness, and itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Leaves: Contain essential oils with antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce fungal infections and soothe skin irritation on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oregano leaves from your garden or store-bought package measuring about one teaspoon.
Rinse the harvested oregano leaves with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine the rinsed oregano leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser allowing it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids completely.
Add honey to taste if desired and enjoy the warm soothing herbal tea immediately while still hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with itchy scalp because of its rich antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalps. Additionally, horsetail's silica content helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve blood circulation, which can alleviate itching caused by conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Horsetail contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and kaempferol, which help reduce inflammation in the scalp and alleviate itchiness.
Antioxidant properties: Horsetail's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to itching and flaking on the scalp.

Soothing astringent properties: Horsetail's astringent properties help dry out and soothe itchy, irritated scalps by reducing moisture and reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal infections that can exacerbate itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, roots help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and irritation.
Stems: High in antioxidants, stems help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin growth, making them effective against itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail leaves and stems from a trusted source measuring about two ounces per cup.
Rinse the harvested horsetail material thoroughly with cold running water removing any debris or dirt.
Combine one ounce of fresh or dried horsetail material with eight ounces of boiling filtered water in a heat-resistant glass infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or up to ten minutes for a stronger tea then strain it.
Enjoy your herbal horsetail tea hot or chilled sweetened if desired and serve within four hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The antioxidants present in dandelion tea also help to reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, the herb's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalps, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Overall, dandelion tea provides a natural and effective solution for alleviating itchy scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, can reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions like psoriasis and eczema.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to scalp irritation and itching.
Soothing and calming effects: The anti-irritant properties of dandelion tea can help calm and soothe an itchy scalp, reducing discomfort and promoting a healthy, balanced scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Contain oils and fatty acids that moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion flowers and leaves from sustainable sources.
Clean the harvested dandelion material by rinsing with cold water and gently pat drying.
Combine one teaspoon of dried dandelion root or two teaspoons of fresh dandelion roots with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes in a covered teapot or infuser to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea and sweeten with honey if desired before serving hot or allowing to cool.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy scalp with herbal teas is by using chamomile or peppermint tea. These teas have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate itching and irritation. Brew a strong tea, let it cool, and then use it as a rinse after shampooing. Gently massage the tea into your scalp, allowing it to sit for several minutes before rinsing. Repeat this process regularly for best results. Additionally, a tea rinse can help maintain a healthy scalp and promote hair growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis. Chamomile tea, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, can help soothe an itchy and irritated scalp. Meanwhile, green tea, rich in antioxidants, can promote hair growth and reduce inflammation. Peppermint tea can help relieve symptoms of dandruff and soothe the scalp due to its cooling and anti-inflammatory effects. It is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for certain scalp conditions, they should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent symptoms. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
